channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Iran team trains through World Cup uncertainty
item.description: Al Jazeera exclusive footage follows Iran’s national football team as they train for the World Cup in Turkiye.

item.title: Turkish police storm main opposition CHP’s party headquarters
item.description: Violence erupted after Turkish police stormed the headquarters of the main opposition CHP party in Ankara.

item.title: China’s Shenzhou-23 astronauts blast off on space station mission
item.description: China’s Shenzhou-23 astronauts blast off on space station mission.

item.title: Iran recounts historic battles in response to Trump’s talk of agreement
item.description: Iran cites battles from history in response to Trump's latest comments on an imminent deal between the US and Iran.

item.title: ‘A paper city’: New York ‘library’ hosts 3.5 million pages of Epstein files
item.description: A US 'library' holding 3.5 million pages of documents attempts to bring accountability for victims of Jeffrey Epstein.

item.title: China sends aid to Cuba amid a harsh US blockade
item.description: China sends aid to Cuba amid a harsh US blockade.

item.title: Bahrain jails nine defendants for life for ‘cooperating’ with Iran’s IRGC
item.description: Convictions come amid an intensified crackdown by Bahraini authorities on individuals accused of having ties to Tehran.

item.title: France bans Ben-Gvir: Which other Israeli leaders have been penalised?
item.description: France bans the Israeli far-right minister after his 'unspeakable' behaviour against arrested Gaza flotilla activists.

item.title: For third year in a row, Israel blocks Hajj pilgrimage for Gaza Muslims
item.description: As millions of Muslims gather in Mecca, Palestinians in Gaza face a third year of heartbreak as Israel has shut border.

item.title: Syria cannot heal without a rebuilt health system
item.description: Many Syrian returnees cannot access proper healthcare. Urgent action is required for Syria’s health system to recover.

item.title: ‘We won’t know who won until final outcome of US-Iran ceasefire’
item.description: ‘We won't know who won until final outcome of US-Iran ceasefire’

item.title: Will the Texas primary run-off give Democrats a chance to flip the state?
item.description: Paxton, the likely Republican nominee, is considered less likely to fare well against Democratic nominee in midterms.

item.title: Iran hints US will end war in ‘Persian-style’ peace on Tehran’s terms
item.description: Iran’s foreign ministry spokesman invoked ancient Persia’s victory over Rome in comparison to today’s US-Iran talks.

item.title: Suicide car bombing attack on a train in Pakistan kills dozens
item.description: A Baloch separatist group has claimed responsibility for an attack on a train carrying soldiers in Quetta, Pakistan.

item.title: Blast hits train in Pakistan’s Balochistan, killing at least 24 people
item.description: More than 50 people injured in attack on train carrying military personnel in Quetta, capital of southwestern province.

item.title: Clashes in Belgrade as student-led protests demand elections
item.description: Demonstrators challenge President Vucic’s rule and demand elections, justice and rule of law.

item.title: Israeli attack on Gaza kills three family members, including infant
item.description: Mohammad Abu Mallouh, ​Alaa Zaqlan and their child, Osama, killed in the attack on Nuseirat refugee camp, medics say.

item.title: The GCC should insure itself against the next Strait of Hormuz crisis
item.description: For that, collective action will be needed now.

item.title: Cape Verde at World Cup 2026: What to know
item.description: After many continental level appearances, Cape Verde are ready for their opening act on football's grandest stage.

item.title: Iran war day 86: Trump announces potential deal amid ‘cloud of mistrust’
item.description: Trump announces progress on Iran peace deal, signalling the Strait of Hormuz, vital for global trade, could reopen soon.

item.title: Senegal football fans return home after royal pardon in Morocco
item.description: Fans arrested in Morocco in the aftermath of AFCON 2025 final were returned on humanitarian pardon by the Moroccan king.

item.title: Trump: No rush on Iran deal, as Netanyahu demands end to nuclear threat
item.description: US blockade remains until final deal, as officials on both sides say obstacles remain

item.title: US, Iran inch closer to deal to end the war: What to know
item.description: The proposed US-Iran peace deal seeks to reopen the strategically vital Strait of Hormuz amid lingering disagreements.

item.title: Russia pounds Ukraine’s capital with hypersonic missile
item.description: Russia pounded Ukraine’s capital overnight on Saturday with drones and ballistic missiles.

item.title: Trump says US and Iran deal to end war is ‘largely negotiated’
item.description: US President Trump announced that a Memorandum of Understanding on a peace deal with Iran has been ‘largely negotiated'.
